LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF MELISSA COPE'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR AN ORDER GRANTING AN EXPEDITED TRIAL - MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THE PARTIES PARTICIPATED DIRECTLY IN EXECUTING THE AGREEMENT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 664.6. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE BREACH OF CONTRACT AND OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION AND VIOLATIONS. IF THE PARTIES SEEK AN ORDER FOR EXPEDITED TRIAL, THEY MUST CONTACT DEPARTMENT 206. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

LAW AND MOTION, 302, MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED AS TO PARAGRAPHS 28-39, 40-68, 138-148 AND AS TO EXHIBIT 4, GRANTED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, AND GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE REMAINING ALLEGATIONS SOUGHT TO BE STRICKEN. THE ENTIRETY OF THE THREE SETS OF PARAGRAPHS SOUGHT TO BE STRICKEN ARE NOT IRRELEVANT OR OTHERWISE IMPROPER. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED GROUNDS FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, BUT IS AFFORDED ONE MORE OPPORTUNITY TO ATTEMPT TO DO SO. PLAINTIFF APPARENTLY CONCEDES THAT THE REMAINING ALLEGATIONS SOUGHT TO BE STRICKEN ARE NOT NEEDED FOR HER CLAIMS AND/OR ARE IMPROPER TO INCLUDE IN HER COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR #5963; DEPT. 302. (302/HK)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT JP MORGAN CHASE BANK N.A.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED ON THE GROUNDS OF STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS AND AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, SIXTH, NINTH AND ELEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION, SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SEVENTH AND TENTH CAUSES OF ACTION, AND SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT DOES NOT UNEQUIVOCALLY SHOW THAT ANY OF THE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE TIME-BARRED. THE FIRST, SECOND, SIXTH, NINTH AND ELEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE ADEQUATELY PLED. THE SEVENTH AND TENTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY AND DECLARATORY RELIEF ARE LEGALLY INSUPPORTABLE IN THIS CONTEXT. THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS DOES NOT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGE EXTREME AND OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT TO SUPPORT SUCH A CLAIM, BUT PLAINTIFF IS AFFORDED ONE MORE OPPORTUNITY TO ATTEMPT TO DO SO. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR #5963; DEPT. 302. (302/HK)

Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-13-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The tentative ruling was accepted by both Counsel. Plaintiff's motion to compel discovery responses to plaintiffs Special Interrogatories, Form Interrogatories, Request for Production of Documents is granted. Plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions is granted in the amount of $1,500.00 as against the defendant only, to be paid within 20 days. The Court will prepare an order. (302/JPT)

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-13-2015 Advanced From May-18-15 To Apr-16-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-13-2015 Advanced From May-18-15 To Apr-16-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-16-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. CHRISTOPHER SARGENT of Computerlaw Group LLP, Tel No. (650) 327-9800 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. JOANNE C. CHAN of Alvarado Smithy APC, Tel No. (415) 624-8665 appearing for Defendant JP Morgan Chase Bank N.A.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court modified its tentative ruling and ordered as follows: DEFENDANT JP MORGAN CHASE BANK N.A.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied as to paragraphs 28-39, 40-68, 138-148 and as to Exhibit 4, granted with ten days leave to amend as to the prayer for punitive damages, and granted without leave to amend as to the remaining allegations sought to be stricken. The entirety of the three sets of paragraphs sought to be stricken are not irrelevant or otherwise improper. Plaintiff has not sufficiently alleged grounds for punitive damages, but is afforded one more opportunity to attempt to do so. Plaintiff apparently concedes that the remaining allegations sought to be stricken are not needed for her claims and/or are improper to include in her complaint. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963 (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-16-2015 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. CHRISTOPHER SARGENT of Computerlaw Group LLP, Tel No. (650) 327-9800 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. JOANNE C. CHAN of Alvarado Smithy APC, Tel No. (415) 624-8665 appearing for Defendant JP Morgan Chase Bank N.A.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT JP MORGAN CHASE BANK N.A.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT, Defendants' demurrer is overruled on the grounds of statute of limitations and as to the first, second, sixth, ninth and eleventh causes of action, sustained without leave to amend as to the seventh and tenth causes of action, and sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the eighth cause of action. The second amended complaint does not unequivocally show that any of the causes of action are time-barred. The first, second, sixth, ninth and eleventh causes of action are adequately pled. The seventh and tenth causes of action for breach of fiduciary duty and declaratory relief are legally insupportable in this context. The eighth cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress does not sufficiently allege extreme and outrageous conduct to support such a claim, but plaintiff is afforded one more opportunity to attempt to do so. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963 (302/HK)

Mtn To Enforce Settlement
Off Calendar Jan-31-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF MELISSA COPE'S MOTION To Enforce Settlement or in the alternative, for an order granting an expedited trial - Motion to enforce settlement is denied. Moving party fails to demonstrate that the parties participated directly in executing the agreement in compliance with CCP 664.6. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to allege breach of contract and other causes of action and violations. If the parties seek an order for expedited trial, they must contact Department 206. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

Ansel Halliburton, Esq. of Computerlaw Group LLP, Tel. 650.327.9800, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was discussed. At the conclusion of the hearing, the court ruled as follows on DEFENDANT JP MORGAN CHASE BANK N.A.'s Demurrer to Complaint: As to Causes of Action 1, 2, 6, 7 and 8, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend; as to Causes of Action 3, 4 and 5, the demurrer is overruled.
